title: Add manifest type conversion to skopeo copy
body:
Add manifest type conversion to skopeo with dir transport

User can select from 3 manifest types: oci, v2s1, or v2s2
Adds option to compress blobs when saving to the directory using the dir transport
e.g skopeo copy --format v2s1 --compress-blobs docker-archive:alp.tar dir:my-directory

Addresses issue https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/issues/361

Signed-off-by: Urvashi Mohnani <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body:
https://github.com/containers/image/pull/377 needs to be merged in first.
I will update the vendor.conf once that is done.

> Man pages?
Completions?

Added man pages and completions

@rhatdan @mtrmac I know we decided to make the default manifest type oci, but wouldn't it be better to let default be whatever the manifest type is from source? Instead of converting it to oci everytime.
Are you able to figure out what the source image type is?  If an image is installed in docker, do we know that it is OCI or V2?

> Are you able to figure out what the source image type is? If an image is installed in docker, do we know that it is OCI or V2?

Yeah containers/image has a function `determineManifestConversion`, which uses the same manifest type as source if a different format is not specified.
Makes sense to duplicate the source, if it can't determine then default to OCI.
LGTM, assuming the merge of https://github.com/containers/image/pull/377 and passing tests.
Updated vendor. Ready to merge once test pass.
Osx build is stuck as usual :(
It would be nice to add papr tests and rh-atomic-bot to skopeo.
Lets get a new version of skopeo out.

> Lets get a new version of skopeo out.

I'll do it by end of day
